entries.summary: npm package manifest for sdd-forge, defining the CLI entry point, published file set, and test scripts.
entries.detail: Declares sdd-forge as an ES module package with the bin entry pointing to src/sdd-forge.js. The files field publishes only src/ while excluding preset test directories. Engines requires Node.js 18+. No runtime dependencies are listed, reflecting the zero-external-dependency policy. Test scripts delegate to tests/run.js with scoping flags for unit, e2e, and acceptance suites.

entries.summary: Top-level dispatcher for the `sdd-forge docs` subcommand group, routing CLI arguments to individual docs pipeline commands.
entries.detail: Parses the first positional argument to select a subcommand (scan, enrich, init, data, text, readme, agents, translate, build, forge, review, changelog). Each handler is dynamically imported at runtime to avoid loading unused modules. The build command orchestrates a full pipeline: scan → enrich → init → data → readme → agents → translate per language. Context is forwarded via baseCtx/langCtx spread objects. Unrecognised subcommands exit with code 1.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge agents` command, which generates and AI-refines the AGENTS.md file for a project.
entries.detail: Reads existing AGENTS.md or creates a skeleton from the base preset template. Resolves `{{data}}` directives from analysis.json using createResolver, then sends the SDD section, project section, docs content, and package.json scripts to an AI agent via callAgent. The refined PROJECT section is written back using replaceProjectContent. Supports --sdd, --project, and --dry-run flags. Throws a structured error when analysis.json is missing.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge changelog` command, generating a Markdown changelog from spec directory entries.
entries.detail: Reads the specs/ directory, sorts subdirectory names, and parses each spec.md for title, status, and date metadata via parseSpecFile and parseDirName. Sanitizes content before writing to docs/change_log.md (or a custom --output path). Supports --lang to load locale config. No AI involvement; purely file-system driven.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge data` command, filling `{{data(...)}}` directives in docs chapters from analysis.json.
entries.detail: Loads analysis.json from the .sdd-forge/output directory and instantiates a resolver via createResolver. Iterates over all chapter files in docs/, calling resolveDataDirectives for each. FILE_CONTEXT_RULES injects per-file context (e.g. relative path) into resolver calls. Changed files are written back; unchanged files are skipped. Exports populateFromAnalysis for use by forge.js and other orchestrators.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge enrich` command, using AI to annotate each analysis entry with summary, detail, chapter, and role metadata.
entries.detail: Collects all analysis entries, splits them into token-bounded batches (default 10 000 tokens), and calls callAgentAsync concurrently (controlled by resolveConcurrency). Each batch prompt includes the chapter list and asks the model to return a JSON array of enriched fields. Responses are parsed with repairJson and merged back into analysis via mergeEnrichment. Progress is saved after each batch; failures dump the raw AI response for debugging. Supports --dry-run (stdout only) and --dump-on-error flags.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge forge` command, the AI-driven multi-round document generation loop.
entries.detail: Supports three modes: local (per-file parallel AI calls), assist (single prompt for all files), and agent (delegates to a sub-agent). Determines target files via getChapterFiles filtered by spec relevance. Each round calls invokeAgent per file concurrently, then runs the review command; if review passes the loop exits early. After writing docs, optionally runs readme and translate. Exposes estimateRelevantFiles for spec-to-file affinity scoring. Exits with EXIT_ERROR if max rounds are exhausted.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge init` command, creating docs chapter files from resolved preset templates.
entries.detail: Resolves the template chain via resolveTemplates and mergeResolved, then translates templates if outputLang differs from the source language using translateTemplate. An optional AI filter (aiFilterChapters) prunes irrelevant chapters based on analysis summary. Detects conflicts with existing files and skips them unless --force is supplied. Creates the docs directory if absent and writes each chapter file. Exports aiFilterChapters for use by forge.js.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge readme` command, generating or updating the project README.md from the docs template.
entries.detail: Resolves the readme template through the preset chain, merges blocks, then resolves `{{data}}` directives via resolveDataDirectives with a custom resolver that injects the README's relative path for link directives. Unfilled `{{text}}` blocks are processed via processTemplate (imported from text.js) using an AI agent. Skips writing if the generated content is identical to the existing file. Accepts --output to redirect to a language-specific README path.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge review` command, validating docs for integrity, unfilled directives, minimum line counts, and analysis coverage.
entries.detail: Checks each chapter file for exposed `{{data}}`/`{{text}}` directives, broken HTML comments, residual block tags, and content below MIN_LINES (15). Also verifies README.md exists, confirms analysis.json is present, validates translated language directories, and checks that enriched entries contain summary/detail. Collects all failures and throws at the end so the forge loop can act on review output. Uses RESIDUAL_BLOCK_RE and checkOutputIntegrity for structural validation.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge scan` command, traversing source files and building analysis.json via registered DataSources.
entries.detail: Loads DataSource plugins from the preset chain's data/ directories, each contributing structured entries (modules, routes, configs, etc.). Computes MD5 hashes of source files and DataSource files to enable incremental re-scan. Existing entries are matched by a composite key (category + file + subkey) to preserve IDs across runs. Supports --reset [category] to clear specific categories. Writes output to .sdd-forge/output/analysis.json and emits it to stdout in --stdout mode.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge text` command, filling `{{text(...)}}` directives in chapter files using AI batch generation.
entries.detail: Groups all unfilled text directives per file into a single batch JSON request, sends them to an AI agent, then applies the JSON response back to the file via applyBatchJsonToFile. Detects which chapters are affected by recent source changes via detectChangedChapters (hash-based). Validates the AI response for unexpected shrinkage (SHRINKAGE_THRESHOLD=0.5 over SHRINKAGE_MIN_LINES). Supports per-directive retries and exports processTemplate/textFillFromAnalysis for use by forge.js and readme.js.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge translate` command, translating docs chapter files and README into target languages via AI.
entries.detail: Builds a task list of (sourcePath, targetPath, lang) pairs, skipping files where the target is newer than the source unless --force is set. Calls callAgentAsync concurrently for each task, passing tone and language instructions via toneInstruction. Uses stripResponsePreamble to clean model preamble from translation output. Writes translated content to docs/<lang>/ subdirectories. Respects concurrency limits from resolveConcurrency.

entries.summary: DataSource providing data for AGENTS.md generation, including the SDD template, config, and package.json scripts.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource and exposes methods for reading the base preset's AGENTS.sdd.md template (language-aware), loading .sdd-forge/config.json, reading package.json scripts, and returning the configured language. Used by agents.js to supply template content and project metadata for directive resolution and AI prompt construction.

entries.summary: DataSource providing documentation structure metadata including chapter listings, navigation links, and language-relative paths.
entries.detail: Reads docs/ chapter files, extracts titles from H1 headers, and builds Markdown tables of chapter links with descriptions. Computes prev/next navigation strings for chapter files. Resolves language-relative paths for cross-language links (docs/<lang>/file.md vs relative paths) based on the calling file's location. Loads language display names from locale/*/ui.json. Reads repository URL from package.json to construct GitHub blob links.

entries.summary: DataSource providing language navigation links (globe icon link list) for multi-language documentation.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource with LANG_NAMES mapping for display names. Computes relative paths between language variants of a doc file and assembles a globe-prefixed Markdown link list (e.g. `🌐 [English](../overview.md) | [日本語](ja/overview.md)`). Path resolution considers whether the file is in the default docs root or a language subdirectory. Loads config via loadJsonFile.

entries.summary: DataSource providing core project metadata (name, description, version) from package.json.
entries.detail: Reads and caches package.json on first access. Exposes projectName (falling back to directory basename), projectDescription, and projectVersion (falling back to '0.0.0'). Builds a Markdown table row for overview sections. Minimal implementation that acts as the authoritative source for package-level project identity fields.

entries.summary: Minimal DataSource stub for text content that always returns null.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource without implementing any data methods, serving as a placeholder registration point for the text category in the DataSource registry. All method calls fall through to the base class defaults. Intended to be replaced or extended if text-specific data retrieval is needed in the future.

entries.summary: Defines the AnalysisEntry base class, ANALYSIS_META_KEYS constant, and utility functions for entry inspection and summarisation.
entries.detail: ANALYSIS_META_KEYS enumerates top-level timestamp keys (analyzedAt, enrichedAt, generatedAt) so consumers can skip them when iterating categories. AnalysisEntry provides a static from() factory that copies plain objects. isEmptyEntry returns true when an entry has no keys beyond COMMON_KEYS (file, hash, lines, mtime). buildSummary aggregates counts and non-empty entry lists per category for prompt construction.

entries.summary: Utility functions for filtering analysis entries by the docsExclude glob patterns defined in config.json.
entries.detail: filterByDocsExclude filters a flat array of entries, converting each glob pattern to a RegExp via globToRegex and excluding entries whose file path matches any pattern. filterAnalysisByDocsExclude applies the same logic across all categories of a full analysis object, skipping ANALYSIS_META_KEYS. Used by enrich.js and data.js to honour per-project exclusion rules.

entries.summary: Resolves chapter ordering and category-to-chapter mappings by merging preset and config chapter definitions.
entries.detail: mergeChapters combines a preset's chapter array with project-level overrides, with the config taking precedence. extractDataCategories scans a template string for `{{data(...)}}` directives and collects referenced categories. buildCategoryToChapterMap inverts the chapter→categories mapping into a category→chapter lookup. buildCategoryMapFromDocs reads all chapter files in docs/ and builds the category map from their actual directives, making the mapping dynamic and authoritative.

entries.summary: Resolves and exposes the shared command context object (paths, config, agent) used by all docs pipeline commands.
entries.detail: resolveCommandContext reads config.json, validates it, resolves root/docsDir/lang/preset/agent fields, and returns a frozen context bag. loadFullAnalysis and loadAnalysisData load analysis.json from the output directory. getChapterFiles returns an ordered list of chapter filenames by consulting preset chapters, config overrides via resolveChaptersOrder, and filtering against the filesystem. readText and stripResponsePreamble are string helpers shared across commands.

entries.summary: Provides mapWithConcurrency, a concurrency-limited async map utility used throughout the docs pipeline.
entries.detail: Implements a slot-based scheduler: maintains a pool of `concurrency` active promises, starts the next item as each slot frees, and collects all results in order. Returns a Promise that resolves to the full results array after all items complete. Used by enrich.js, forge.js, text.js, and translate.js to control parallel AI agent calls.

entries.summary: Dynamically loads DataSource plugin instances from a directory of .js files.
entries.detail: Iterates .js files in a dataDir, imports each as an ES module, instantiates the default export (expected to be a DataSource subclass) with root and docsDir arguments, and registers it under the file basename as a key. Attaches _sourceFilePath for hash-based change detection in scan.js. Returns the accumulated sources map after processing all files.

entries.summary: Base class for all DataSource implementations, providing the method dispatch protocol and Markdown table helper.
entries.detail: The primary entry point is the unnamed dispatch method (called by the resolver) that routes to _desc(section, key) returning '—' by default. withOverrides wraps an item array so each item's summary can be replaced by a project-local override loaded via _loadOverrides. toMarkdownTable formats a rows array into a pipe-delimited GFM table with a separator row. Subclasses override individual methods to supply domain-specific data.

entries.summary: Parses and manipulates `{{data(...)}}`, `{{text(...)}}`, and block/extends template directives in Markdown files.
entries.detail: parseDirectives performs a full line-by-line scan producing a list of directive descriptors (type, line range, fields). resolveDataDirectives iterates directives and calls resolveFn for each data block, replacing content in-place. replaceBlockDirective and replaceInlineDirective handle the two syntactic forms. stripBlockDirectives removes block/extends lines without altering content. parseBlocks extracts named blocks and extends declarations for template inheritance in template-merger. findClosingTag throws on unclosed directives.

entries.summary: Builds AI prompt strings for the forge command: system prompt, per-file prompt, and analysis summary serialisation.
entries.detail: summaryToText delegates to enrichedSummaryToText which formats analysis categories into a structured text block listing each entry's file, summary, and detail for inclusion in prompts. buildForgeSystemPrompt constructs the top-level system instruction combining language, user prompt, spec content, and analysis summary. buildForgeFilePrompt generates a per-file round prompt with review feedback and round counter. buildForgePrompt is the combined full-context variant used in assist mode.

entries.summary: Factory module that maps file extensions to language-specific handler objects (js, php, py, yaml). Returns the appropriate handler for a given file path via getLangHandler().
entries.detail: Maintains an EXT_MAP dictionary keyed by file extension (e.g., .js, .php, .py, .yml, .yaml). The getLangHandler function extracts the lowercased extension and looks it up in the map, returning null for unsupported types. Acts as a single dispatch point so callers never need to branch on file type themselves.

entries.summary: JavaScript language handler providing parse, minify, extractImports, extractExports, and extractEssential operations on JS source text. Used by the scanner and minifier pipeline.
entries.detail: Implements comment stripping (block and line), indent normalization, and minification. The parse() function extracts className, parentClass, methods, and relations from JS source using regex. extractEssential() filters to lines that reference fs, path, JSON, process, or child_process APIs to produce a condensed representation. Exports follow the standard lang handler interface consumed by lang-factory.js.

entries.summary: PHP language handler mirroring the JS handler interface, with additional hash-comment stripping specific to PHP. Parses class name, parent class, methods, properties, and relations from PHP source.
entries.detail: Strips block comments, line comments, and PHP-style hash comments before minification. The parse() function uses regex to detect class declarations, inheritance, method signatures, and property visibility. extractImports/extractExports follow the shared lang handler contract. removeHashComments handles inline and full-line # comments while preserving URL-style double-slash sequences.

entries.summary: Python language handler with hash-comment removal and extractEssential filtering. Implements the minimal lang handler interface required by lang-factory.
entries.detail: Provides removeHashComments() to strip # comments from each line and a minify() function that delegates to it. extractEssential() filters lines to produce a condensed representation. Does not implement parse() or extractImports/extractExports, reflecting Python's simpler scan requirements in the current pipeline.

entries.summary: YAML language handler providing only hash-comment stripping and minification. Implements the minimal lang handler interface.
entries.detail: Contains removeHashComments() and a minify() function identical in shape to the Python handler. YAML files do not need parse or import/export extraction, so no other functions are exported. Registered in lang-factory's EXT_MAP for .yml and .yaml extensions.

entries.summary: Top-level minifier that dispatches to language-specific handlers via lang-factory and applies shared blank-line and trailing-whitespace removal. Entry point for all code minification in the docs pipeline.
entries.detail: The minify() function retrieves the appropriate lang handler for the given filePath and calls either extractEssential() (when opts.essential is set) or the handler's minify(). Falls back gracefully when no handler exists. removeBlankLines and removeTrailingWhitespace are applied as post-processing steps to produce compact output for prompt context.

entries.summary: Utility library for parsing PHP array literals: strips comments, extracts array bodies and keys, parses quoted strings, and provides camelToSnake and pluralize helpers.
entries.detail: stripBlockComments removes /* */ style comments. extractArrayBody locates a named PHP property array by bracket counting and returns the raw body string. extractTopLevelKeys and extractQuotedStrings use regex scanning on the body. camelToSnake converts camelCase identifiers for CakePHP conventions. pluralize handles simple English pluralization with capital-letter awareness. Re-exported by scanner.js for wider use.

entries.summary: Factory that constructs a resolver object combining preset chain data sources, project overrides, and a desc() helper for looking up override values. Central wiring point for the data pipeline.
entries.detail: loadDataSources wraps the base loader with resolved context. loadOverridesFor reads overrides.json from the project's .sdd-forge directory and caches it. descFactory returns a curried function that checks overrides before falling back to '—'. createResolver resolves multi-chain presets, loads their data sources, and returns a resolver with resolve(), resolveAll(), and listSources() methods. Handles async loading and missing data gracefully.

entries.summary: Parses AI review output text to extract unanswered questions, generate a summary of failures, and classify files as passed or failed. Used by the review pipeline to process agent responses.
entries.detail: extractNeedsInput scans output for question-formatted lines and returns non-empty entries. summarizeReview picks the first failed or ambiguous file verdict as a representative summary. parseFileResults matches output lines against a list of expected files by basename comparison, splitting results into passedFiles and failedFiles sets.

entries.summary: Provides the Scannable mixin that adds scan-source capability to DataSource base classes. Defines the contract for scannable entries including a default empty-entry factory.
entries.detail: Scannable is a class factory (mixin pattern) that wraps a Base class and adds a boolean scan-guard and a newEntry() factory method returning a fresh Entry instance via this.constructor.Entry. Allows DataSource subclasses to opt into incremental scanning without duplicating the interface.

entries.summary: Core file scanning utilities: pattern matching, file stat collection, recursive directory walking, and language-aware file parsing. Foundation for the sdd-forge scan pipeline.
entries.detail: patternToRegex and globToRegex convert glob/pattern strings to RegExp. getFileStats reads file content and metadata (hash, mtime, size). findFiles and collectFiles walk directory trees applying include/exclude rules and return sorted relative-path lists. parseFile dispatches to the correct lang handler via getLangHandler. parsePHPFile and parseJSFile are legacy-compatible wrappers. Re-exports camelToSnake and pluralize from php-array-parser.

entries.summary: Template merging engine that resolves layered preset templates, merges additive and override blocks, and supports multi-language fallback and AI-assisted translation. Core of the docs generation pipeline.
entries.detail: buildLayers constructs an ordered list of template directories from the preset chain. resolveOneFile merges sources from all layers using parseBlocks for directive-aware block merging. mergeResolved supports both additive and override strategies. resolveTemplates discovers all template files across chains with fallback. translateTemplate calls an AI agent to convert template content between languages. discoverFileNames orders chapter files according to chaptersOrder config. resolveChaptersOrder merges preset-defined and config-overridden chapter ordering.

entries.summary: Detects test framework environment from analysis data by checking for known test framework signatures. Returns presence flag, detected frameworks list, and inferred test command.
entries.detail: Defines TEST_FRAMEWORKS as a list of known framework identifiers to match against analysis entries. detectTestEnvironment iterates the analysis to find matching entries and derives the appropriate test command string. Returns a structured result used by text-prompts and other documentation generators to include testing context.

entries.summary: Builds AI prompts for documentation text generation, including system prompts, per-directive prompts, batch prompts, and enriched source context. Central prompt construction library for the text pipeline.
entries.detail: getAnalysisContext assembles structured analysis data keyed by ANALYSIS_META_KEYS for injection into prompts. getEnrichedContext reads source files, applies minification, and produces enriched context with a character limit. buildTextSystemPrompt constructs the base system prompt from documentStyle and language settings. buildPrompt and buildBatchPrompt format per-directive and multi-directive generation requests. buildFileSystemPrompt appends serialized analysis JSON to the base system prompt. formatLimitRule derives conciseness instructions from directive parameters.

entries.summary: Minimal TOML parser supporting string, array, and key-value pair extraction. Used to parse configuration files in TOML format within the docs pipeline.
entries.detail: parseTOML iterates lines to build a result object, handling section headers and key=value assignments. parseValue distinguishes quoted strings, arrays, and bare values. parseArray handles comma-separated bracketed lists with basic string tokenization. Does not support nested tables or multi-line strings, covering only the subset of TOML needed by sdd-forge.

entries.summary: Main dispatcher for the `sdd-forge flow` subcommand. Resolves project context, routes to registered command handlers, and manages process exit codes.
entries.detail: Parses argv to extract the command group and subcommand. resolveCtx loads repo root, config, flow state, and worktree info, exiting with EXIT_ERROR on missing context when required. Dispatches through the FLOW_COMMANDS registry, calling runEntry which wraps async handlers and re-throws errors. Top-level await drives the async dispatch loop.

entries.summary: Implements the `flow merge` command, determining whether to create a GitHub PR or perform a squash merge based on config and gh availability. Parses spec content to build PR title and body.
entries.detail: resolveRemote reads the push remote from config. isGhAvailable checks if the gh CLI binary is present. parseSpec extracts title, requirements, and acceptance criteria sections from spec markdown. buildPrTitle and buildPrBody compose PR metadata from flow state and parsed spec. The main() function evaluates commands.gh config and gh availability to select strategy: 'pr', 'squash', or 'skip'.

entries.summary: Generates and persists flow execution reports by aggregating per-phase metrics from flow state. Used as a shared utility by both the report command and finalize.
entries.detail: aggregateMetrics sums counters (question, redo, docsRead, srcRead) across all phases into totals. generateReport constructs a structured data object and a human-readable text summary via formatText. saveReport writes report.json to the spec directory alongside other spec artifacts. formatText produces a markdown-style lines-joined summary of metrics and phase breakdowns.

entries.summary: Implements the full code review workflow including draft review, final verdict with AI proposals, and test review with gap analysis and automated fix application. The most complex flow command.
entries.detail: resolveReviewTarget gathers spec text and related implementation files. Draft and final review phases build distinct system prompts and parse structured proposals. Test review runs up to MAX_TEST_REVIEW_RETRIES cycles of gap analysis: buildTestDesignPrompt generates a test design, buildGapAnalysisPrompt identifies missing coverage, and buildTestFixPrompt produces fixes applied by applyTestFixes. formatReviewMd and formatTestReviewMd produce persisted review artifacts. extractRequirements isolates the requirements section from spec text.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get check`, validating whether preconditions for a given target (impl, finalize, dirty, gh) are met. Returns structured pass/fail results for each check.
entries.detail: PREREQS maps target names to required prior step completions. checkStepPrereqs iterates required steps and evaluates their status in flow state. checkDirty runs git status to detect uncommitted changes. checkGh tests gh CLI availability. execute selects the validator based on the target argument and returns an envelope with per-check pass/fail details and a summary string.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get context`, providing file reading, analysis search, and keyword collection operations for AI context building. Supports ngram, AI, and fallback search modes.
entries.detail: execute routes to file-read, analysis-dump, search, or keyword-list modes. ngramSearch computes bigram similarity scores against all analysis entries. aiSearch uses an AI agent to select relevant keywords before filtering entries. fallbackSearch applies simple substring matching. filterEntry removes excluded metadata fields before returning entries. toSearchResult normalizes entries to a consistent shape. collectAllKeywords gathers all keywords from analysis sorted by frequency.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get guardrail`, returning guardrail rules for a specified phase in text or JSON format. Loads merged guardrails from preset chain and filters by phase.
entries.detail: parseGuardrailArgs extracts the positional phase argument and optional --json format flag. toMarkdown formats guardrail entries as a markdown list. execute calls loadMergedGuardrails and filterByPhase, then outputs via the flow envelope. VALID_PHASES restricts accepted phase values to draft, spec, impl, and lint.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get issue`, fetching a GitHub issue by number and returning its content as a JSON envelope. Delegates to the gh CLI.
entries.detail: execute extracts the issue number from ctx.args, calls execFileSync with gh issue view --json, parses the JSON response, and wraps it in an ok envelope. Fails with an appropriate error message if the gh call throws or the issue number is missing.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get prompt`, returning predefined prompt text by language and kind. Provides a registry of reusable prompt strings for flow phases.
entries.detail: PROMPTS_BY_LANG is a nested object keyed by language code then prompt kind. validKinds() returns the list of available kinds for the default language. execute looks up the prompt by lang and kind from ctx, returning it in an ok envelope or failing with a list of valid kinds on mismatch.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get qa-count`, returning the count of QA items from the current flow state. Simple read accessor for flow metrics.
entries.detail: execute reads the qa array from flow state and returns its length in an ok envelope. Returns zero when no QA entries exist. Used by skill scripts to determine whether the QA phase has populated items before proceeding.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get resolve-context`, assembling a comprehensive context snapshot including worktree status, branch info, spec content, and active flow state. Used by skills to orient AI agents.
entries.detail: execute determines whether to use a worktree or main repo path, resolves the active flow spec, reads spec and relevant section text via extractSection, and gathers git state (branch, ahead count, last commit, worktree status). Outputs a structured envelope combining all these fields. Handles both specId-based and active-flow-based resolution paths.

entries.summary: Implements `flow get status`, returning the current flow phase and step statuses derived from flow state. Minimal read accessor for the flow lifecycle.
entries.detail: execute calls derivePhase on the flow state steps to compute the current phase and wraps it with the full steps array in an ok envelope. Used by skill scripts and the status display command to show progress through the SDD workflow.

entries.summary: Central registry mapping all flow command strings to their handler modules with step-tracking middleware. Defines the complete command surface of the flow subsystem.
entries.detail: FLOW_COMMANDS is a flat object where each key is a 'group/command' string mapping to an entry with execute, requiresFlow, pre, and post hooks. stepPre and stepPost generate middleware closures that call updateStepStatus before and after execution. deriveActivePhase reads current phase from persisted flow state. The registry pattern decouples flow.js dispatch from individual command implementations.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run finalize`, orchestrating the final steps of an SDD flow: report generation, worktree cleanup, and optional docs sync. Entry point for post-implementation teardown.
entries.detail: STEP_MAP defines the sequence of finalize sub-steps. executeCleanup handles spec-only mode, worktree removal, and git branch cleanup. execute resolves which steps to run based on CLI flags, calls generateReport and saveReport, conditionally runs the docs build script via runSync, and delegates cleanup. Uses PKG_DIR to locate the docs.js script.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run gate`, validating a spec file against structural rules and AI-evaluated guardrails. Acts as a quality gate before implementation begins.
entries.detail: checkSpecText applies regex-based structural checks, skipping certain sections defined in PRE_SKIP_SECTIONS. buildGuardrailPrompt formats guardrail rules and spec text into an AI prompt. parseGuardrailResponse extracts pass/fail verdicts from the AI response. checkGuardrail orchestrates both checks, returning combined results. execute reads the spec from the path in flow state and reports issues via the flow envelope.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run impl-confirm`, displaying a summary of changed files and pending requirements to help the AI agent confirm readiness for implementation.
entries.detail: getChangedFiles runs git diff against the base branch to list modified paths. summarizeRequirements parses the requirements array from flow state into counts of done, pending, and in-progress items. execute assembles the confirmation payload including spec existence, changed files, and requirement summary, returning it in an ok envelope for the skill to present.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run lint`, running lint checks using merged guardrail rules from the preset chain. Delegates to the shared runLint utility.
entries.detail: execute loads merged guardrails via loadMergedGuardrails, parses CLI flags for target file or directory, calls runLint with the guardrail set, and wraps the result in a flow envelope. Supports dry-run mode. Shares guardrail loading logic with the gate command.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run prepare-spec`, creating the spec directory, branch, worktree, spec.md, and qa.md files that initialize a new SDD flow. The entry point for starting a new feature spec.
entries.detail: nextIndex scans the specs directory to assign a sequential spec number. slugify normalizes the request text to a valid branch name. ensureClean and ensureBaseBranch validate the repo state. createSpecTemplate and createQaTemplate generate initial file content, falling back to local templates in .sdd-forge/templates. writeFlowState persists the initial flow.json. Worktree creation and branch setup use runGit helpers.

entries.summary: Entry point for the `flow run report` command, loading the redo log and delegating to generateReport/saveReport. Thin wrapper over the shared report library.
entries.detail: execute reads the spec directory from flow state, loads the redo log via loadRedoLog, and passes metrics and redo data to generateReport. Calls saveReport to persist report.json and returns the changed file path in the ok envelope.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run retro`, performing an AI-assisted retrospective comparing implemented diff against requirements. Produces a structured retro.json artifact.
entries.detail: extractRequirements locates the requirements section in spec text. getDiff and getDetailedDiff run git diff against the base branch. buildRetroPrompt constructs an AI prompt including requirements and diff context. parseRetroResponse extracts structured pass/fail verdicts per requirement from the AI JSON response. execute validates preconditions, calls the AI agent, and writes retro.json to the spec directory.

entries.summary: Thin runner for `flow run review`, delegating to the flow/commands/review.js script via runSync and parsing its output. Handles both code review and test review modes.
entries.detail: execute builds the script path from PKG_DIR, assembles args from ctx, and calls runSync. parseTestReviewOutput inspects the exit code and stdout/stderr to construct appropriate ok or fail envelopes. Passes through --test, --dry-run, and other flags from ctx.args.

entries.summary: Implements `flow run sync`, running the docs build pipeline from within a flow worktree to synchronize documentation with current source code.
entries.detail: execute resolves the docs.js script path via PKG_DIR and calls it through runSync with the appropriate project root. Passes --lang and other config flags derived from flow state. Returns an ok envelope on success or a fail envelope capturing stderr output.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set auto`, toggling the autoApprove flag in flow state to enable or disable automatic step progression.
entries.detail: execute parses the boolean value from ctx.args and calls mutateFlowState to update the auto field. Returns an ok envelope with the new auto value. Used by the /sdd-forge.flow-auto-on and /sdd-forge.flow-auto-off skills.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set issue`, storing a GitHub issue reference in flow state to associate the flow with a tracked issue.
entries.detail: execute extracts the issue number from ctx.args and calls setIssue to persist it in flow.json. Returns ok with the stored issue value or fail if the argument is missing. Used during the spec phase to link implementation work to its originating issue.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set metric`, incrementing named counters (question, redo, docsRead, srcRead) for a specified flow phase in flow state.
entries.detail: VALID_PHASES and VALID_COUNTERS define allowed values. execute validates both the phase and counter arguments, then calls mutateFlowState to increment the targeted counter within the metrics object for that phase. Returns ok with the updated counter value or fail with validation errors.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set note`, appending a text note to the notes array in flow state. Used to record observations and decisions during a flow.
entries.detail: execute extracts the note text from ctx.args and calls addNote to append it to flow.json. Returns ok with the added note or fail if no text was provided. Notes are surfaced in status output and the context resolver for AI agent awareness.

entries.summary: Manages the redo log (redolog.json) tracking revision history entries within a flow. Provides loadRedoLog and saveRedoLog utilities plus the `flow set redo` command.
entries.detail: loadRedoLog reads redolog.json from the spec directory, validating that entries is an array, and returns an empty structure if the file does not exist. saveRedoLog writes the updated log with JSON formatting. execute appends a new redo entry with timestamp and reason from ctx.args and persists via saveRedoLog.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set req`, updating the status or text of a specific requirement entry in flow state by index.
entries.detail: execute extracts the requirement index and new value from ctx.args and delegates to updateRequirement which mutates flow.json. Returns ok with the updated requirement or fail on invalid index. Used by skill scripts to mark requirements as done or in-progress during implementation.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set request`, storing the original user request text in flow state for reference throughout the flow lifecycle.
entries.detail: execute extracts the request string from ctx.args and calls setRequest to persist it in flow.json. Returns ok with the stored request or fail if empty. The request is used by skills to maintain context about what triggered the current flow.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set step`, updating the status of a named step in flow state. Allows manual step progression outside of automatic pre/post hooks.
entries.detail: execute parses the step ID and status value from ctx.args, validates the status against allowed values, and calls updateStepStatus to persist the change. Returns ok with the updated step or fail on invalid arguments. Used by skill scripts to advance or revert step states.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set summary`, storing a structured requirements array in flow state parsed from JSON or plain text input.
entries.detail: execute attempts JSON.parse on the raw input; on failure, treats the input as plain text and wraps it in a single-item array. Calls setRequirements to persist the parsed requirements in flow.json. Returns ok with the stored requirements or fail on empty input.

entries.summary: Implements `flow set test-summary`, recording a test execution summary (pass/fail counts and type) in flow state after test runs.
entries.detail: TYPE_FLAGS maps --unit, --integration, --acceptance to type strings. execute parses the type flag and numeric pass/fail arguments from ctx.args and calls setTestSummary to persist in flow.json. Returns ok with the summary or fail on missing arguments. Used by skill scripts after running the test suite.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge help` command, displaying a formatted list of available commands with their i18n-translated descriptions.
entries.detail: LAYOUT defines the ordered list of command groups and entries. main() maps entries to name/desc pairs using the translate function keyed by ui:help.commands.*. runIfDirect ensures main() only executes when the file is run directly. Exports both main and the LAYOUT constant for reuse in other help contexts.

entries.summary: Core agent invocation library providing synchronous and asynchronous callAgent functions, agent config resolution, context file management, and stdin-based prompt passing for large inputs.
entries.detail: buildAgentInvocation constructs the final argv and environment for the agent process, switching to stdin delivery when prompt size exceeds ARGV_SIZE_THRESHOLD. callAgent uses execFileSync for synchronous calls; callAgentAsync returns a Promise with optional retry logic via callAgentAsyncWithRetry. resolveAgent merges provider profile args with command-specific overrides from config. writeAgentContext writes prompt context to .claude/rules/ files for structured injection. ensureAgentWorkDir validates and creates the working directory within the project root.

entries.summary: Loads the SDD section template from the base preset's AGENTS.sdd.md file for a given language. Used during AGENTS.md generation to inject the SDD template block.
entries.detail: loadSddTemplate resolves the template path under PRESETS_DIR/base/templates/<lang>/AGENTS.sdd.md and reads it if present, returning an empty string as fallback. Called by the agents command to obtain the canonical SDD section content before AI augmentation of the PROJECT section.

entries.summary: Core CLI utility library providing repo root resolution, source root detection, argument parsing, worktree detection, and package metadata access.
entries.detail: PKG_DIR resolves the package root from import.meta.url for locating bundled scripts. repoRoot checks SDD_WORK_ROOT env first, then falls back to git rev-parse. sourceRoot checks SDD_SOURCE_ROOT for source-target separation. parseArgs implements a spec-driven flag parser supporting boolean, string, and multi-value flags. isInsideWorktree detects git worktrees by checking if .git is a file rather than a directory. getMainRepoPath reads the gitdir file to resolve the main repo path from a worktree.

entries.summary: Configuration loading and path resolution utilities for sdd-forge projects. Loads, validates, and provides typed access to .sdd-forge/config.json.
entries.detail: sddDir, sddConfigPath, sddOutputDir, and sddDataDir compute canonical .sdd-forge subdirectory paths from project root. loadConfig reads config.json and passes it through validateConfig for schema enforcement. loadLang provides a quick language-only read with DEFAULT_LANG fallback. loadJsonFile is a generic JSON file reader with existence checking. DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY and DEFAULT_LANG define system defaults.

entries.summary: Provides isDirectRun and runIfDirect utilities to guard top-level script execution, ensuring modules only self-execute when invoked directly rather than imported.
entries.detail: isDirectRun compares the resolved argv[1] path with the module's own file path via fileURLToPath. runIfDirect wraps a main() function in a try/catch, exiting with EXIT_ERROR on failure. Used across commands/scan.js, review.js, changelog.js, and others to prevent side effects on import.

entries.summary: Defines numeric exit code constants EXIT_SUCCESS (0) and EXIT_ERROR (1) used consistently across all CLI entry points.
entries.detail: A minimal constants module with two named exports. Centralizing exit codes prevents magic number scatter and makes process.exit() calls searchable. Imported by flow.js, review.js, gate.js, and numerous other command files.

entries.summary: Defines the flow envelope protocol with ok(), fail(), warn(), and output() functions for structured JSON communication between flow commands and their callers.
entries.detail: ok() constructs a success envelope with type, key, data, and empty errors. fail() constructs a failure envelope with error code and messages array. warn() combines a data payload with non-fatal warning codes. output() serializes the envelope to stdout as pretty-printed JSON and sets process.exitCode (not process.exit()) to allow post-hooks to run. This protocol is the universal return format for all flow command handlers.

entries.summary: Manages persistent flow state for SDD workflows, including loading, saving, and mutating flow.json files per spec. Also handles active flow tracking across worktrees and scanning all flows in the repository.
entries.detail: Defines FLOW_STEPS and PHASE_MAP constants to drive workflow progression, and exposes helpers to derive the current phase from completed steps. Supports multi-worktree scenarios by maintaining a shared .active-flow index file in the main repo's .sdd-forge directory. Provides atomic mutation via mutateFlowState(), and metric tracking via incrementMetric() for phase-level counters. cleanStaleFlows() removes entries whose spec directories or worktrees no longer exist, preventing dangling references.

entries.summary: Provides lightweight utility functions for reading Git repository state such as branch name, dirty files, ahead count, and last commit. Also checks whether the gh CLI is available.
entries.detail: All functions delegate to execFileSync via a tryExec wrapper that returns null on failure, making every call safe to use without try/catch at the call site. getWorktreeStatus() parses git status --porcelain output to return a dirty flag and list of modified files. getAheadCount() counts commits ahead of a base branch using git rev-list. The module has no side effects and no persistent state.

entries.summary: Loads, filters, and merges guardrail rules from JSON files across the preset chain and project-level overrides. Guardrails define linting constraints applied during specific SDD phases.
entries.detail: Each guardrail entry is hydrated with a parsed lint regex via hydrate() before use. filterByPhase() selects rules applicable to the current workflow phase, defaulting to the spec phase. matchScope() checks whether a file path matches a guardrail's scope glob or filename pattern. loadMergedGuardrails() walks the preset inheritance chain via resolveChainSafe() and merges rules by ID, with project-level overrides taking precedence over preset defaults.

entries.summary: Implements the i18n translation system, loading message files from locale directories in the package, preset, and project layers. Exposes createI18n() and translate() factory functions that return a typed t() lookup function.
entries.detail: Messages are organized by domain (ui, messages, prompts) and merged in priority order: package locale < preset locale < project locale. deepMerge() performs recursive object merging so individual keys can be overridden without replacing entire domain files. interpolate() handles {{key}} placeholders within message strings. translate() is a lazy factory that defers locale dir resolution until first call, reading config and preset from the project root.

entries.summary: Resolves <!-- include("path") --> directives in template content by reading referenced files and substituting their contents inline. Enforces path traversal restrictions and detects circular includes.
entries.detail: resolveIncludePath() maps include paths to absolute locations using a priority chain: templatesDir, presetsDir, pkgDir, then baseDir. Path segments starting with ../ or ./ are explicitly forbidden to prevent directory traversal. Circular include detection is implemented via a visited Set passed recursively through resolveIncludes(). Included files are themselves processed for nested includes, and baseDir is updated to the directory of the included file for relative resolution.

entries.summary: Provides a repairJson() function that tolerates malformed JSON (unquoted keys, trailing commas, markdown fences) and returns a valid JSON string. Used to parse LLM-generated JSON responses.
entries.detail: The parser is a hand-written recursive descent that processes characters one by one, emitting corrected output. It handles objects, arrays, strings, numbers, and keywords (true/false/null) with error recovery rather than failure. stripMarkdownFences() strips ```json ... ``` wrappers before parsing, and findJsonStart() locates the first { or [ to skip leading prose. escapeControl() ensures control characters inside strings are properly escaped as \uXXXX sequences.

entries.summary: Executes lint guardrail checks against changed files in the repository and returns structured pass/fail results. Integrates with the guardrail system for phase-aware rule selection.
entries.detail: getChangedFiles() uses git diff to enumerate files modified relative to a base branch. runLintChecks() iterates over files, reads their content, and evaluates each guardrail's regex against the file, recording failures with file path, rule ID, and matched line. runLint() orchestrates the full pipeline: filter guardrails by phase, collect changed files, run checks, and return a result object with ok flag, warning list, and failure details. validateLintGuardrails() pre-screens guardrail definitions for missing required fields.

entries.summary: Renders an interactive terminal tree-based single/multi-select UI for choosing presets during setup. Supports keyboard navigation and ANSI color output.
entries.detail: buildTreeItems() walks the preset tree recursively to produce flat items with indentation prefix strings for visual hierarchy. renderLine() applies ANSI color codes to highlight the cursor position and selected items differently for single vs. multi-select modes. select() sets up raw stdin input, handles arrow key sequences and space/enter events, manages viewport scrolling, and resolves the Promise with the selected key(s) on confirmation. Falls back to the first item when stdin is not a TTY.

entries.summary: Discovers all available presets from the src/presets/ directory and provides chain resolution for preset inheritance. Exposes the PRESETS constant and helpers to look up or traverse the parent chain.
entries.detail: discoverPresets() reads each subdirectory for a preset.json manifest and builds a list of { key, dir, parent, label } objects. resolveChain() follows the parent field recursively to build an ordered inheritance chain, detecting circular references and missing parents with explicit errors. resolveMultiChains() merges chains for multiple preset types, deduplicating entries by key. resolveChainSafe() wraps resolveChain() to return an empty or base-only array on failure, used in contexts where missing presets should not abort execution.

entries.summary: Thin wrapper around Node.js spawnSync that returns a normalized result object with stdout, stderr, status, and error fields. Used throughout the codebase for synchronous subprocess execution.
entries.detail: runSync() sets encoding to utf8 and merges caller-provided options, then returns spawnSync's result without throwing. This design lets callers inspect stderr and exit status explicitly rather than catching exceptions. The module has no state and no side effects beyond spawning a child process.

entries.summary: Implements a terminal progress bar and spinner with multi-step tracking, elapsed time display, and verbose logging. Renders to stderr to avoid polluting stdout output.
entries.detail: createProgress() returns an instance with step(), log(), warn(), and done() methods that update a shared progress bar rendered as an ASCII block bar. A setInterval-driven spinner animates the current step while it runs. initScreen() clears the terminal and reserves lines for the header and bar using ANSI escape codes when stderr is a TTY; non-TTY environments fall back to plain line output. createLogger() provides a simpler prefix-tagged logging interface without progress tracking.

entries.summary: Deploys SKILL.md files from the package templates into the project's .agents/skills/ and .claude/skills/ directories. Processes include directives before writing and skips files that are unchanged.
entries.detail: deploySkills() reads skill template files from PKG_DIR/templates/skills/, resolves <!-- include() --> directives with the full template and preset context, and writes the resolved content to both agent skill directories. Existing symlinks are unconditionally replaced to handle upgrades from older symlink-based deployments. Files whose content has not changed are skipped to avoid unnecessary writes. Results are returned as an array of { name, status } records indicating created, updated, or skipped.

entries.summary: Defines config validation logic for SddConfig objects and resolves the output configuration structure from a raw config. Serves as the single source of truth for required config fields.
entries.detail: validateConfig() checks for required fields (lang, type, docs, docs.languages, docs.defaultLanguage) and accumulates all errors before throwing, giving the user a complete list of problems at once. resolveOutputConfig() derives the output directory paths and language settings from a validated config, abstracting away the nested docs structure for consumers. VALID_TONES constrains the docs.style.tone field to a fixed set of allowed values.

entries.summary: CLI entry point for the sdd-forge presets subcommand. Prints a tree view of available presets including their template directories.
entries.detail: The script reads process.argv[2] to dispatch between subcommands (currently only the default tree view). printTree() iterates over PRESETS and calls printNode() recursively to render the hierarchy with prefix strings and template presence indicators. Exits with EXIT_ERROR for unknown subcommands.

entries.summary: Main entry point for the sdd-forge CLI. Dispatches subcommands to namespace dispatchers (docs, flow) or independent command scripts.
entries.detail: Parses the first argument to determine whether to invoke a namespace dispatcher (docs.js, flow.js) or a standalone script listed in the INDEPENDENT map. For help and version flags, it handles them inline before any dispatch. process.argv is rewritten before dynamic import so that each command module sees correct argv. Exits with EXIT_ERROR for unrecognized subcommands, providing a clean error boundary at the top level.

entries.summary: Interactive setup wizard that initializes a new sdd-forge project by collecting configuration, creating directory structure, writing config.json, and deploying skills and agent config files.
entries.detail: runWizard() prompts the user for project name, source path, language, preset type, and docs purpose/tone using readline-based ask() helpers. buildTreeItems() and select() drive an interactive preset picker in the terminal. ensureProjectDirs() creates .sdd-forge/, docs/, and specs/ directories. ensureGitignore() and ensureGitattributes() patch root-level git configuration files. ensureAgentConfigFile() injects the SDD directive block into AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md, and deploySkills() copies skill templates into .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/.

entries.summary: Implements the sdd-forge upgrade command, which refreshes skill files and optionally patches config.json to remove deprecated fields. Designed for running after package updates.
entries.detail: main() resolves the project root via repoRoot(), loads the existing config with loadConfig(), and calls deploySkills() to update .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/ from the current package templates. Deprecated config keys are removed from the raw JSON and the file is rewritten if changes were made. Exits with EXIT_ERROR if config loading fails, providing a safe upgrade path that does not overwrite user customizations.
